symbols: Holy Family resting in a vast landscape — the refugee family trusting God's protection
expansive landscape — the dangerous journey through a hostile world
river and distant cliffs — the passage through peril to safety
description: A wide panoramic landscape with the small figure of the Virgin Mary seated with the infant Christ in the middle ground, resting beside a path. Dense trees provide shade. A vast landscape stretches out behind them with rivers, rocky cliffs, villages, and distant mountains under a hazy sky. The figures are dwarfed by the immense natural setting, characteristic of Patinir's world-landscape style.
theological_description: The Flight into Egypt (MAT.2.13-15) follows the angel's warning to Joseph: 'Rise, take the child and his mother, and flee to Egypt, and remain there until I tell you, for Herod is about to search for the child, to destroy him.' This event fulfills Hosea's prophecy: 'Out of Egypt I called my son' (HOS.11.1). The vast landscape in Patinir's rendering emphasizes the vulnerability and courage of the Holy Family — refugees fleeing political violence with only divine guidance to protect them. The journey echoes Israel's own sojourn in Egypt and exodus, establishing Jesus as the new Moses who will lead a greater liberation. The rest along the way connects to the broader biblical theme of God providing respite for his weary people: 'Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest' (MAT.11.28).

scripture_refs.reason: The angel's command to flee to Egypt, the direct subject
Out of Egypt I called my son, fulfilled in Jesus's return from Egypt
Herod's massacre of the innocents, the danger they flee
Come to me and I will give you rest, reflected in the resting scene

_meta.title: Landscape with The Flight into Egypt
